text: Inside this glass-walled space, I feel a curious duality—at once both observer and observed. The design of the exhibit hints at innovation and research, evoking thoughts about humanity's restless desire to understand and shape the world. The colors are vibrant, yet the environment is sterile—a place of inquiry rather than comfort. My mind is drawn to questions of purpose and progress; I sense anticipation tinged with detachment, as though I am present for the unveiling of something new, yet not yet part of its story. The juxtaposition of openness and enclosure makes me wonder about the boundaries between participation and observation.
